method: Use small multiples bar charts with districts on x-axis and yield on y-axis, grouped by season (Kharif/Rabi). Include interactive filters for crops. Coordinated tooltips show production metrics. Color encoding distinguishes seasons.
objective: Compare seasonal productivity patterns across districts to identify high/low performance regions

method: Create a sortable table with lollipop charts showing top/bottom 5 districts per crop. Columns: District, Crop, Yield. Interactive sorting triggers animated transitions. Highlight extremes using diverging color scales.
objective: Identify districts with maximum/minimum yields for specific crops to inform best practices replication

method: Scatterplot matrix combining district-aggregated soil nutrients (D3) and yields. Brush selections update maps showing spatial distribution. Trend lines show polynomial regression relationships.
objective: Analyze relationships between soil composition and crop productivity at district level

method: Box plots showing yield distributions per district with hover details. Integrated MAD-based outlier detection marks points beyond 3σ. Parallel coordinates plot links outliers with cultivation area metrics.
objective: Detect anomalous yield patterns requiring investigation into cultivation practices or data quality

method: Apply k-means clustering to group districts by yield/production metrics across seasons. Radial visualization encodes cluster characteristics. Dynamic PCA projection aids cluster interpretation.
objective: Discover groups of districts with similar production profiles to enable regional policy planning

method: Scrollable heatmap (V4) showing water-yield ratios for top-10 crops. Columns: Irrigation methods, rows: Crops. Color intensity represents yield per liter consumed. Linked histograms show distribution details.
objective: Optimize irrigation strategies by identifying water-efficient crops and methods
